Japan Weighs $3 Billion Power Subsidies as LNG Crunch Bites
headlines.summary: The Japanese government is considering a round of subsidies for electricity for a period of three months, Reuters reported today, citing unnamed sources. The price tag of the subsidies is seen at the equivalent of $3.1 billion. If approved, the subsidies will take effect in July

headlines.title: Brent Breaks $113 as Traders Brace for Months of Disruption
headlines.summary: Oil prices continued to climb in early Asian trade on Thursday, with traders pricing in a prolonged disruption to Middle East supply flows as inventories continue to shrink. headlines.title: California Refineries Max Out Jet Fuel While Gasoline Starves
headlines.summary: California is one of the places where fuel prices have long traded above global averages, but the current crisis is pushing that premium into uncharted territory. headlines.title: Oil Rallies Toward $120 As Middle East Supply Risks Spiral
headlines.summary: Oil prices extended their rally on Wednesday after reports emerged that the U.S. will tighten its blockade on Iranian ports, prolonging fears of reduced oil supply from the region, and as the UAE hit markets with its OPEC-exit announcement. Brent crude for June delivery jumped 6.

headlines.title: Hormuz Blockage Boosts Panama Canal Oil Tanker Traffic
headlines.summary: Oil tanker traffic through the Panama Canal has jumped in recent weeks as Asia scrambles to buy U.S. crude amid choked supply from the Middle East. headlines.title: Venezuelan Crude Will Eventually Lower US Gas Prices: Chevron Exec

headlines.title: Another Russian Oil Facility Struck As Zelensky Touts Drone Capabilities
headlines.summary: A major fire reportedly broke out on Wednesday at a Russian oil pumping station in the Perm region in the Ural Mountains in a suspected drone attack just a day after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy touted the increasing range and accuracy of Ukrainian drones. headlines.title: Pakistan PM: Oil Import Costs Up 167% Since Iran War Began
headlines.summary: Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced on Wednesday that the country’s oil import bill has jumped 167% to $800 million per week amid the war in Iran from around $300 million weekly before the conflict began. headlines.title: Phillips 66 Beats Q1 Estimates by $0.88 Per Share as Refining Margins Surge
headlines.summary: In an earnings season highlighted by a Middle East conflict that has sent oil prices soaring, Texas oil refiner Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) has reported first quarter adjusted earnings of $0.49 per share, easily beating Wall Street's consensus of a loss of $0.39 while net income came

headlines.title: EIA: US Crude Oil Inventories Crashing, But Holding Above Average
headlines.summary: Crude oil inventories in the United States decreased by 6.2 million barrels during the week ending April 24, according to new data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) released on Wednesday.
